name: Confirm Transaction
desc: Server confirms or rejects an inventory transaction from C0EPacketClickWindow. accepted=false causes client to revert inventory.

fields.name: actionNumber
fields.desc: Transaction action number from the click packet

fields.name: accepted
fields.desc: Whether the server accepted the click

notes: If accepted=false, the client empties and refills its inventory by re-reading all slots from the server (C30PacketWindowItems is re-sent). This is how the server corrects desynced client inventory.

implementation.overview: S32PacketConfirmTransaction is the server's response to every C0EPacketClickWindow. It carries the window ID, action number (short), and a boolean accepted flag. If accepted=false, the client must revert its entire inventory — which the server re-sends as S30PacketWindowItems. This packet is HEAVILY targeted by Disabler modules (67+ module refs) and Backtrack modules. Aqua 13's Backtrack stores S32 in its replay buffer alongside entity movement packets, adding artificial latency to incoming transactions.

modules.name: Backtrack (Aqua 13)
modules.purpose: Delay incoming S32 packets (and others) to create a temporary 'past state' buffer. When KillAura is active, the module holds packets for up to 2000ms and replays them, making the player appear at an earlier position for hit detection.
modules.how_it_works: Backtrack's blockPacket() method checks if each incoming packet should be buffered. S32PacketConfirmTransaction is one of the many packet types stored in the replay queue (line 225). When the buffer fills or a timer expires, ALL queued packets are replayed in order via processPacket(). This creates the 'backtrack' effect where the entity's past position is extended for hit registration.
modules.vanilla_hook: NetHandlerPlayClient.handleConfirmTransaction(S32PacketConfirmTransaction) — when accepted=false, clears and refills inventory slots via S30PacketWindowItems.
modules.anti_cheat_notes: Backtrack delaying S32 transaction confirmations can cause inventory desync — if S32 is held for 1000ms, the client-side inventory won't match server-side. The player may see phantom items or missing items for the duration of the backtrack. Some anticheats check that S32 responses arrive within a specific time window — delayed transactions may trigger 'transaction timeout' flags.

modules.name: Bloom / Tenacity Disabler (S32 Cancel)
modules.purpose: Cancel or queue S32PacketConfirmTransaction to prevent the server from tracking inventory states. Used by Watchdog disabler modes that queue C0F + C00 together.
modules.how_it_works: The Disabler cancels incoming S32, preventing the client from reverting inventory on rejected transactions. By blocking S32, the client can maintain an inconsistent inventory state that breaks anticheat tracking.
modules.anti_cheat_notes: Cancelling S32 causes the client's inventory to stop matching the server. Items may appear to duplicate or vanish locally.

implementation.general_hooks:
Client: NetHandlerPlayClient.handleConfirmTransaction(S32PacketConfirmTransaction). Aqua 13 Backtrack buffers it. Disabler cancels it.

Server: Sent after every C0EPacketClickWindow. If server-side slotClick produces a different result than client-side, accepted=false.
implementation.client_variations:
Aqua 13: Backtrack buffers S32 in replay queue (2000ms max). Disabler imports S32 for transaction manipulation.
Rise 6.0: Backtrack buffers entity packets but exempts S32. Disabler cancels S32 in some modes.
Tenacity 5.1: Disabler queues S32 + C00 for Watchdog burst mode.
RektSky B5: No specific S32 module.
Sigma 4.11: EventPacket handles S32.
Memeware 7.3: PacketInEvent for S32.
Jigsaw 0.26: Vanilla handler only.
